title: Speaking - Part 2
description: Compare the two photographs and answer the question. You have about one minute.

tasks.instructions: Compare the two photographs and say what challenges wildlife conservation faces and what motivates people to protect endangered species.

images.label: A
images.description: A wildlife ranger monitoring a tranquilised elephant as vets remove an illegal poaching snare, a helicopter visible in the background

images.label: B
images.description: A marine biologist in full scuba gear attaching a tracking tag to a sea turtle beneath the surface of a coral reef

followUp.question: Do you think zoos and captive breeding programmes are an effective way to protect endangered species? What are the arguments for and against?

tasks.instructions: Compare the two photographs and say what the consequences of biodiversity loss might be and how human activity threatens or protects natural habitats.

images.label: D
images.description: Aerial view of a vast area of rainforest that has been clear-cut for agriculture, a sharp line between dense green jungle and barren brown earth

images.label: E
images.description: A display in a natural history museum dedicated to extinct animals, including a life-size model of a dodo and a thylacine, visitors looking sombre

followUp.question: Do you think individuals or governments are more responsible for protecting biodiversity? What actions should each take?
